title: oldenlandia corymbosa var. caespitosa
intro: Oldenlandia corymbosa var. caespitosa, commonly known as the creeping oldenlandia, is a low-growing, herbaceous plant native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities. The plant is known to possess various health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from respiratory issues, and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of the creeping oldenlandia are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
